paper_map.title: Work hardening in Fe–Al alloys
paper_map.abstract_summary: Work hardening in polycrystalline Fe–Al alloys with Al content up to 40 at.% shows a sharp maximum near 26–30% Al. The peak correlates with a transition from single to superdislocation dipoles, predominance of straight screw dislocations, and formation of sessile dislocation segments. Elastic anisotropy peaks in the same composition range but is believed to reflect core/Peierls effects rather than being the direct cause.
paper_map.research_objective: To understand the origin of the pronounced work hardening peak in Fe–Al alloys as a function of Al content and associated ordering transitions.
main_process_variables: as-cast/annealed state (thermally relaxed, coarse grains, no solute/precipitate effects)
slow cooling for ordering (moderate DO3 order)
boron addition (~0.1% B) in some alloys
